editorial_summary: Healthcare AI takes a decisive turn toward real deployment this week. Anthropic doubles down on life sciences with a $400M acquisition while launching cybersecurity partnerships that matter to health systems. Meanwhile, the prior authorization reform that everyone celebrated last summer delivered an underwhelming 11% reduction, and AI scribes are officially driving up healthcare costs. The gap between AI hype and healthcare reality is widening, but the winners are becoming clear.

Prior authorization reform is falling flat at just 11% reduction despite industry pledges, while AI integration accelerates at the point of care with Ambience launching nursing copilots and Anthropic dropping $400M on biotech. Health systems are caught between underwhelming payer reforms and the pressure to deploy autonomous AI to survive margin pressures.

Healthcare AI is hitting an execution wall. While everyone talks about Shadow AI and nurses get their first real EHR-integrated copilots, the industry's bigger challenge is becoming clear: we're great at pilots, terrible at scale. Meanwhile, Anthropic's $400M biotech bet and prior auth reform's measly 11% progress show the gap between AI hype and healthcare reality.

Three big shifts define this week: AI scribes are officially driving up healthcare costs (everyone agrees, nobody knows what to do), prior auth reform is delivering pathetic results at just 11% reduction, and consulting firms are making aggressive AI plays with Anthropic's $400M biotech bet and Chartis acquiring Leap AI. Meanwhile, the nursing AI revolution gets real with Ambience's EHR-integrated copilot at Cleveland Clinic.
